proof_sketch: blockDim.y=1 from kernel launch (block_size=256). For the fixed model configuration (dim likely 1 or 2), inner_size <= 256, so grid_y = ceil(inner_size/256) = 1. Thus blockIdx.y is always 0. Both original and mutated inner_idx evaluate to threadIdx.x. No value variation can make them differ.

With original, each thread gets a unique inner_idx from 0 to inner_size-1, covering all elements. With mutated, inner_idx ranges from 0 to (grid_y-1) + (block_size-1).
